metadata.name: pykitti
metadata.summary: A minimal set of tools for working with the KITTI dataset in Python

modules.name: pykitti.utils
documentation: Provides helper methods for loading and parsing KITTI data.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.rotx
documentation: Rotation about the x-axis.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.roty
documentation: Rotation about the y-axis.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.rotz
documentation: Rotation about the z-axis.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.transform_from_rot_trans
documentation: Transforation matrix from rotation matrix and translation vector.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.read_calib_file
documentation: Read in a calibration file and parse into a dictionary.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.pose_from_oxts_packet
documentation: Helper method to compute a SE(3) pose matrix from an OXTS packet.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.load_oxts_packets_and_poses
documentation:
Generator to read OXTS ground truth data.

Poses are given in an East-North-Up coordinate system 
whose origin is the first GPS position.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.load_image
documentation: Load an image from file.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.yield_images
documentation: Generator to read image files.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.load_velo_scan
documentation: Load and parse a velodyne binary file.

functions.name: pykitti.utils.yield_velo_scans
documentation: Generator to parse velodyne binary files into arrays.

modules.name: pykitti
documentation: Tools for working with KITTI data.

modules.name: pykitti.odometry
documentation: Provides 'odometry', which loads and parses odometry benchmark data.

documentation: Read RGB stereo pair at the specified index.

Load and parse odometry benchmark data into a usable format.

modules.name: pykitti.tracking
documentation: Provides 'tracking', which loads and parses tracking benchmark data.

functions.name: pykitti.tracking.to_array_list
documentation:
Converts a dataframe to a list of arrays, with one array for every unique index entry.
Index is assumed to be 0-based contiguous. If there is a missing index entry, an empty
numpy array is returned for it.
Elements in the arrays are sorted by their id.
\:param df:
\:param length:
\:return:

classes.name: pykitti.tracking.KittiTrackingLabels

methods.name: pykitti.tracking.KittiTrackingLabels._split_on_reappear
documentation:
Assign a new identity to an objects that appears after disappearing previously.
Works on `df` in-place.
\:param df: data frame
\:param p: presence
\:param id_offset: offset added to new ids
\:return:

Kitt Tracking Label parser. It can limit the maximum number of objects per track,
filter out objects with class "DontCare", or retain only those objects present
in a given frame.
